title: selaginella temehaniensis
intro: Selaginella temehaniensis, also known as Tahitian fern or Polynesian spike moss, is a species of spore-producing vascular plant that belongs to the family Selaginellaceae. It has been traditionally used in Polynesian medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Selaginella temehaniensis include its ability to reduce inflammation and pain, improve respiratory health, and support immune function. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional Polynesian medicine to treat a range of ailments.
